metadata.title: Complex Healthcare Sector Cyber Attack Scenario
metadata.author: John Doe

overview.brief_summary: A sophisticated cyber attack targeting the healthcare sector, utilizing advanced tactics and techniques to infiltrate and disrupt operations.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Malware Repository Scheduled Task Application Log File User Account Process Network Traffic DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method Artificial Intelligence Malware Metadata (Mechanism) Process Injection Scheduled Task/Job Application Log Content (Mechanism) File Metadata (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses artificial intelligence to gather information about potential targets, including healthcare organizations and their employees.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker creates custom malware tailored to target specific healthcare organizations and their employees.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails to healthcare employees, tricking them into revealing sensitive information or installing malware.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker compromises healthcare employees' devices by visiting malicious websites.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker injects malware into processes running on compromised systems to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to execute malware at set intervals, ensuring continued access to compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker gains elevated privileges on compromised systems by exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing applications.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker hides malware code from detection by obfuscating command-line arguments.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker attempts to guess login credentials using brute-force methods.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps network infrastructure by monitoring network traffic.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally within the network using remote services.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates stolen data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts healthcare operations by disabling network hardware.

Scenario.summary: This complex cyber attack scenario involves multiple stages, from reconnaissance and resource development to impact. The attacker uses various techniques, including spearphishing, drive-by compromise, process injection, and denial-of-service attacks, to infiltrate and disrupt healthcare operations.
